question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user access GET /api/v1/contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash and observe the function_parameters field to decode the ABI-encoded arguments passed to the delegated contract, determining the exact parameters the signer authorized and using them to craft an identical unauthorized call?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve the authorization_list from GET /api/v1/contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash and observe that the address field points to a known malicious contract, indicating that the signer was tricked into authorizing code delegation to an attacker-controlled contract, and use this information to identify other victims of the same attack?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user query GET /api/v1/contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash with multiple nonce values (nonce=0&nonce=1) to retrieve authorization_list data from multiple related EIP-7702 transactions in a single request, enabling bulk harvesting of signer delegation signatures without any ownership check?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the value field in the response of GET /api/v1/contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash reveals the HBAR/ETH amount transferred during an EIP-7702 delegated execution, enabling them to identify high-value delegations and prioritize replay attacks against signers who authorized large transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/contractRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getContractResultsByTransactionIdOrHash()] Can an unprivileged external user access GET /api/v1/contracts/results/:transactionIdOrHash and observe the contract_id field to identify which contract executed the EIP-7702 delegation, then query GET /api/v1/contracts/:contractId to retrieve the contract's bytecode and analyze it for vulnerabilities that could be exploited through the signer's delegation?
